mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Conversely persistent increases compress valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its 12-month trailing minimum (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), this signals labor market deterioration consistent with recession onset, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.7 as of latest snapshot, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE breaches, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of FEDFUNDS relative to year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation approximates the real policy rate; when this real rate is high and rising (nominal rate far above inflation), tight real monetary conditions suppress equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward SPX returns. The effect requires both series moving together, not either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=5.06 with CPIAUCSL YoY moderating as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC of the constructed real-rate proxy against 20-day forward returns is not significantly different from the single-series FEDFUNDS-only model (delta IC < 0.01) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in HOUST during a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS elevated above 4.5%) signals interest-rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In easing or neutral rate regimes the same HOUST decline carries weaker predictive power.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1401.0 and FEDFUNDS=5.06 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered HOUST declines under high FEDFUNDS are not significantly more negative than unconditional HOUST-decline returns (p > 0.10), indicating the regime conditioning adds no value.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and business momentum, which is incorporated by equity markets with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO trend and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year growth in RSAFS decelerates below 2%, signaling weakening consumer spending which drives roughly two-thirds of GDP, forward-looking equity markets price in reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following triggered RSAFS deceleration are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth alongside an uptick in UNRATE signals a turning point in the labor cycle that precedes broader earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone (payrolls slowing with stable unemployment, or unemployment ticking up with stable payrolls) is sufficient to trigger the effect.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=156105.0 and UNRATE=3.7 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction signal produces Rank IC no greater than the best single-series model (delta IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS plateaus after a tightening cycle (three consecutive months with no increase after prior hikes), markets interpret the pause as reduced tail risk of further tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This effect is specific to the late-cycle tightening-pause regime and should not appear during active hiking or active cutting phases.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3 consecutive months following a prior hiking sequence
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=5.06 as of latest snapshot, near cycle peak
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during triggered FEDFUNDS pause periods are not significantly higher than returns during active hiking periods (t-test p > 0.05).
